title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: At which value of x is f continuous but not differentiable?

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: The table shows values of $f'$, the derivative of function $f$. Although $f'$ is continuous over all real numbers, only selected values of $f'$ are shown. If $f'$ has exactly two real zeros, then $f$ is increasing over which of the following intervals?

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: Given that the second derivative of f is f''(x) = (x - a)^2(x - d), at which labeled value(s) of x does the graph of f have an inflection point?

title: SCEMQA 开放题
rewritten_question_text: Shown in the diagram is a graph of $f'$, the derivative of function $f$. If $f(3) = 3$, then $f(1)=$?

title: SCEMQA open-ended
rewritten_question_text: Shown is a graph of $f'$, the derivative of function $f$. Complete the statement: $f$ is ______ from $x=-3$ to $x=-1$.
